Conference Registration FormΔ
Conference Registration

FOR LOCAL ATTENDEES RESIDING IN PAPUA NEW GUINEA

Welcome to the registration for the LAWASIA Environmental Law Conference 2026. 

This site is for local attendees only, from PNG. If you are an international attendee (not from PNG), please visit the International Registration Page here: https://lawasia.eventsair.com/lawasia-environmental-law-conference2026/standard-registration/Site/Register

 All registrations must be completed online.

Important: Registration is limited to first 100 local participant and will be allocated on a first-come, first-served basis.

Participants are advised to affect their own insurance to cover all risks including (but not limited to) costs of travel, accommodation, registration, injury / death, health and medical expenses, personal property and all other risks. Pacific Judicial Officers’ Handbook

Pacific Judicial Officers’ Handbook on Fraud and Corruption

This Handbook was produced by the Federal Court of Australia as part of the Pacific Judicial Integrity Program. It includes laws, procedures, jurisprudence, interpretation, and conclusions intended to assist Pacific judges presiding over fraud and corruption-related cases. The information should not be taken to represent the views of the Federal Court of Australia, the countries it concerns, or the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade that funded its development. The information should not be regarded as legal advice or used as a substitute for independent legal research.

Efforts have been made to ensure that the information in the Handbook is accurate and up to date. However, the Federal Court of Australia does not guarantee or warrant the accuracy, completeness, or currency of the information provided. The Federal Court of Australia does not assume responsibility from any use, reliance on, or failure to use the information provided.

title: Integrity and Judicial Well-being
excerpt_text: UNODC and the Papua New Guinea Judiciary present Agenda Agenda International Judicial Conference on Integrity and Judicial Well-being Agenda International Judicial Conference on Integrity and Judicial Well-being Background As a follow up to the Regional Judicial Conference held in Nauru from 24 to 26 July 2024 and the adoption of the Nauru Declaration on Judicial […]
content_text:
UNODC and the Papua New Guinea Judiciary present

				Agenda

							Agenda

						International Judicial Conference on Integrity and Judicial Well-being

					Background

									As a follow up to the Regional Judicial Conference held in Nauru from 24 to 26 July 2024 and the adoption of the Nauru Declaration on Judicial Well-being on 25 July 2024, the Papua New Guinea Judiciary and UNODC and Commonwealth Secretariat will host the International Conference on Judicial Integrity and Judicial Well-being from 20 to 22 August 2025 in Port Moresby. This conference will be supported by the Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence and UNODC under the framework of the Pacific Platform on Anti-Corruption: The Teieniwa Vision and aligned with the objectives of the United Nations Convention Against Corruption (UNCAC), particularly article 11, which underscores the importance of enhancing integrity within the judiciary.
Building on the goals of the Global Judicial Integrity Network, established by UNODC in 2018 and on the outcomes of the Regional Judicial Conference held in Nauru from 24 to 26 July 2024, the conference will bring together judges, judicial authorities, and experts from around the world, with a particular emphasis on the Pacific region. Participants will discuss emerging trends and challenges related to judicial integrity and well-being.
Key topics to be explored during the conference include Judicial Integrity and Judicial Well-being, including how they are interconnected and strengthen one another. The event aims to identify additional challenges, strengthen existing networks, and deepen insights into the needs of judges and judiciaries, fostering a more robust and integrity-driven judicial system.

					Objective:

					Key Themes:

									The central objective of the conference is to facilitate a focused discussion and exchange of ideas on judicial well-being and integrity. We aim to promote a deeper understanding of the psychological and technological challenges and opportunities facing the judiciary, and to develop strategies to enhance judicial resilience, ethical decision-making, and overall well-being, including by identifying the parameters of the drafting commentary of Nauru Declaration on Judicial well-being.

									- Nauru declaration on judicial well-being
- Interplay between judicial integrity and well-being of judges
- Psychological aspects of judicial well-being, judicial bullying and harassment
- Judicial leadership and well-being
- Technology and judicial well-being

					Expected Outcomes:

									The purpose of the event is to bring together high-level representatives of judiciaries and through expression of interest younger generation magistrates and judges to discuss existing and emerging challenges in the region and beyond:
- Enhanced Understanding: Participants will gain a deeper understanding of the psychological and technological challenges facing the judiciary and their impact on judicial well-being and integrity. This will include a focus on mental health, stress management, and the role of leadership in fostering a culture of well-being, as well as the opportunities technology offers for improving efficiency and reducing stress.
- Actionable Strategies: The conference will generate practical recommendations and strategies for enhancing judicial resilience, ethical decision-making, and overall well-being. This will include developing strategies for implementing judicial well-being programs, addressing mental health challenges, and using technology effectively to support judicial functions.
- Nauru Declaration Implementation Plan: Participants will work towards identifying the parameters for drafting the commentary on the Nauru Declaration on Judicial Well-Being. This plan will outline specific actions, guidelines, and recommendations for implementing the declaration’s key principles, ensuring a global commitment to improving judicial well-being across the judiciaries around the world.
- Strengthened Collaboration and Networks: The event will foster stronger regional and international collaboration among judiciaries, encouraging the exchange of best practices, support mechanisms, and initiatives.

					Format:

					Participants:

									The conference will feature plenary sessions, panel discussions, and interactive sessions to encourage active participation and exchange of ideas among participants. Keynote speakers and subject matter experts will provide insights and guidance on the conference themes.

									The conference will bring together judges from around the world. Facilitators and experts will be drawn from both regional and global levels.

The event aims to identify additional challenges, strengthen existing networks, and deepen insights into the needs of judges and judiciaries, fostering a more robust and integrity-driven judicial system.

Objective:

Key Themes:

The central objective of the conference is to facilitate a focused discussion and exchange of ideas on judicial well-being and integrity. We aim to promote a deeper understanding of the psychological and technological challenges and opportunities facing the judiciary, and to develop strategies to enhance judicial resilience, ethical decision-making, and overall well-being, including by identifying the parameters of the drafting commentary of Nauru Declaration on Judicial well-being.

  • Nauru declaration on judicial well-being
  • Interplay between judicial integrity and well-being of judges
  • Psychological aspects of judicial well-being, judicial bullying and harassment
  • Judicial leadership and well-being
  • Technology and judicial well-being

Expected Outcomes:

The purpose of the event is to bring together high-level representatives of judiciaries and through expression of interest younger generation magistrates and judges to discuss existing and emerging challenges in the region and beyond:

  • Enhanced Understanding: Participants will gain a deeper understanding of the psychological and technological challenges facing the judiciary and their impact on judicial well-being and integrity. This will include a focus on mental health, stress management, and the role of leadership in fostering a culture of well-being, as well as the opportunities technology offers for improving efficiency and reducing stress.
  • Actionable Strategies: The conference will generate practical recommendations and strategies for enhancing judicial resilience, ethical decision-making, and overall well-being. This will include developing strategies for implementing judicial well-being programs, addressing mental health challenges, and using technology effectively to support judicial functions.
  • Nauru Declaration Implementation Plan: Participants will work towards identifying the parameters for drafting the commentary on the Nauru Declaration on Judicial Well-Being. This plan will outline specific actions, guidelines, and recommendations for implementing the declaration’s key principles, ensuring a global commitment to improving judicial well-being across the judiciaries around the world.
  • Strengthened Collaboration and Networks: The event will foster stronger regional and international collaboration among judiciaries, encouraging the exchange of best practices, support mechanisms, and initiatives.

Format:

Participants:

The conference will feature plenary sessions, panel discussions, and interactive sessions to encourage active participation and exchange of ideas among participants. Keynote speakers and subject matter experts will provide insights and guidance on the conference themes.

The conference will bring together judges from around the world. Facilitators and experts will be drawn from both regional and global levels.

Pacific Judicial conference, New Zealand

[/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=”2/3″ css=”.vc_custom_1740452052952{margin-top: 20px !important;}”][vc_column_text]

Pacific Centre For Judicial Excellence

[/vc_column_text][vc_column_text]The Pacific Island Countries Centre for Judicial Excellence was formed from the amalgamation of activities under the Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence (PNGCJE) and the need for Judicial Education and Training Programs for the pacific Islands developed within the Pacific.

Since the inception on PNGCJE in 2010 it was envisaged that it would build on judicial programs domestically and then extend to the Pacific Islands placing emphasis on their specific requirements.

PCJE has been developing in three phases namely the strengthening on the institutional capacity on PNGCJE which includes establishing professional staff, arranging transitional requirements and the transformation on PNGCJE to become PCJE.

PCJE plays an integral part in assisting the judiciaries in the Pacific region in building capacity through structured training programs for judicial officers, Judges, Magistrates, Court Officers and officers in the Law and Justice Sector.

The countries represented in the Pacific Islands Region include Cook Islands, Fiji, Federated States of Micronesia, Kiribati, Marshall Island, Nauru, Niue, Palau, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tokelau, Tonga, Tuvalu and Vanuatu.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][vc_column width=”1/3″][vc_btn title=”Training Program Schedule” style=”outline” shape=”square” color=”warning” size=”lg” align=”center” skin=”primary” button_block=”true” css=”.vc_custom_1625010758397{padding-top: 70px !important;}”][vc_btn title=”Member Countries” style=”outline” shape=”square” color=”warning” size=”lg” align=”center” skin=”primary” button_block=”true”][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1740963694002{margin-top: 40px !important;}”][vc_column]

content_text:
[vc_row css=”.vc_custom_1604464967634{margin-top: -20px !important;}”][vc_column]CUSTOMER SERVICE CHARTER
[vc_separator color=”orange”][vc_tta_pageable no_fill_content_area=”1″ autoplay=”4″ active_section=”1″ pagination_color=”orange” css=”.vc_custom_1604888252270{margin-top: -20px !important;margin-bottom: -40px !important;background-color: #ffffff !important;}”][vc_tta_section title=”Section 1″ tab_id=”1604452577694-c35702f1-cbb9″][vc_cta h2=”MISSION” h2_font_container=”font_size:25|color:%23000000″ h2_google_fonts=”font_family:Francois%20One%3Aregular|font_style:400%20regular%3A400%3Anormal” h4_font_container=”font_size:25″ h4_google_fonts=”font_family:Fanwood%20Text%3Aregular%2Citalic|font_style:400%20regular%3A400%3Anormal” txt_align=”center” use_custom_fonts_h2=”true” use_custom_fonts_h4=”true” css=”.vc_custom_1624420052166{margin-bottom: -40px !important;padding-bottom: 50px !important;background-color: #f9f9f9 !important;}”]Promote a high standard of Excellence in all Court Business.[/vc_cta][/vc_tta_section][vc_tta_section title=”Section 2″ tab_id=”1604452577694-5569ed56-c13f”][vc_cta h2=”VISION” h2_font_container=”font_size:30|color:%23000000″ h2_google_fonts=”font_family:Francois%20One%3Aregular|font_style:400%20regular%3A400%3Anormal” h4_font_container=”font_size:25″ h4_google_fonts=”font_family:Fanwood%20Text%3Aregular%2Citalic|font_style:400%20regular%3A400%3Anormal” txt_align=”center” use_custom_fonts_h2=”true” use_custom_fonts_h4=”true” css=”.vc_custom_1624419960735{margin-bottom: -45px !important;background-color: #f9f9f9 !important;}”]An Avenue where Experts come together to share knowledge, skills and expertise to be the best in their roles, enabling access to Justice and public confidence in the Judicial system.[/vc_cta][/vc_tta_section][/vc_tta_pageable][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=”1/3″][vc_separator color=”orange”]PURPOSE

The charter entails the service delivery process that Judges, Magistrates and Court staff can observe when engaging in our training programs.

[vc_separator color=”orange”][/vc_column][vc_column width=”1/3″][vc_separator color=”orange”]OUR OBJECTIVE

Your Professional development is our Business, we will strive to ensure that all Judicial Officers, magistrates, court staff and officers of the Law and Justice sector are equipped with sound knowledge and prepared to manage the unique challenges of their Judicial role.

[vc_separator color=”orange”][/vc_column][vc_column width=”1/3″][vc_separator color=”orange”]OUR SERVICE

We plan, design and deliver training programs, seminars, workshops, forums, conferences and webinars for Judges, Magistrates and Court Officers.

[vc_separator color=”orange”][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_tta_tabs color=”orange” spacing=”” active_section=”1″ css_animation=”none” no_fill_content_area=”true”][vc_tta_section title=”WHAT TO EXPECT” tab_id=”1604363215320-335a84e4-85bf”][vc_cta h2=”At PngCJE, we are prepared:”]

- To meet with you and discuss your training needs

- To develop Curriculum, plan, design and deliver training programs

- To Monitor and evaluate your progress after training

- To Provide State of the Art Training facilities

- To support you transition into your new career and constantly monitor your Professional development

- To engage Subject Matter Experts to plan, design and deliver tailored training programs for your specific needs

[/vc_cta][/vc_tta_section][vc_tta_section title=”OUR CLIENTS” tab_id=”1604363215320-574d8109-9d1d”][vc_cta h2=”PngCJE services are extended to:”]

- Judges;

- Magistrates;

- Judges associates, court interpreters, attendants and reporters;

- Registry staff;

- Sheriff’s officers and staff;

- Corporate management staff;

- Law and Justice Sector agencies staff engaged in Court processes;

- Village Courts Magistrates and Lay or non-lawyer trained Court Officers;

- Regional (Pacific Island Countries) and international Judges and court support staff; and

- Other stakeholders on ad hoc basis, i.e. lawyers, public, legislature, executive and non- judicial members of administrative tribunals and school children/students.

[/vc_cta][/vc_tta_section][/vc_tta_tabs][vc_separator color=”orange”]OUR PROGRAM OFFICERS

You will find our Program Officers useful in facilitating your training programs. They are more than happy to assist in designing and delivering training programs to suit your training needs. Feel free to send an email to any of the following;

[vc_images_carousel images=”1455,1454,1450,1449,1452,1451,1453″ img_size=”1100×1500″ onclick=”custom_link” custom_links_target=”_blank” speed=”2500″ slides_per_view=”4″ autoplay=”yes” hide_pagination_control=”yes” partial_view=”yes” wrap=”yes” custom_links=”#E-8_” css=”.vc_custom_1663719593394{margin-top: 50px !important;margin-bottom: 50px !important;margin-left: 10px !important;border-radius: 2px !important;}”][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=”1/2″][vc_separator color=”orange”]PngCJE Board of Directors

The PngCJE Board of Directors chaired by the Chief Justice of Papua New Guinea examine and approve all training programs planned by PngCJE.

[vc_separator color=”orange”][/vc_column][vc_column width=”1/2″][vc_separator color=”orange”]MOST IMPORTANTLY

We are committed to doing our very best in delivering our mandated task to provide judicial education for court staff and Judicial officers for your Professional development.

We want our court officers to be well equipped with the necessary skills, knowledge and attitudes in dispensing justice for the wellbeing and development of the society at large.

[vc_separator color=”orange”][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_cta h2=”FEEDBACK”]Your feedback on our service is our tool for perfection, help us serve you better. Compliments or complaints can be sent to us through the following channels

- https://pngcje.gov.pg/ Click and send us a comment via ‘Contact Us’ on our website

- Fill out our evaluation forms after every training activity;

- Send an email to our Program Officers or;

- You can simply give us a call, +675 324 5500

We will review and renew this customer service charter regularly to meet and exceed your expectations.

PngCJE is ultimately responsible for your Professional development, if you are not satisfied with the outcome of our service, please do not hesitate to contact us. We will ensure that we respond to your concern in a timely manner.[/vc_cta][vc_separator color=”orange”][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row]

Its aim is to deliver structured training programs for Judges, […]
content_text:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_custom_heading text=”Governance” font_container=”tag:h2|font_size:36|text_align:left” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][vc_separator color=”custom” align=”align_left” border_width=”3″ type=”small” accent_color=”#f27a3e”][vc_column_text]The Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence was established in 2010 under a Memorandum of Understanding (‘MoU’) entered into between the Chief Justice, Chief Magistrate and the Secretary for the Department of Justice and Attorney General. Its aim is to deliver structured training programs for Judges, Magistrates, Court officers and other officers of the Law and Justice Sector agencies that play a part in the Court process.

Established under the MoU is a Board which governs PngCJE with membership from all law and justice sector agencies that are concerned with judicial administration and development. PngCJE has a Secretariat that is headed by an Executive Director who also serves as the Chief Executive officer and Secretary to the Board.

The MoU for the establishment of PngCJE also provided an organizational structure which has been implemented during the term of the first PngCJE Business Plan. The structure consists of a Board, a Secretariat and a Faculty of Trainers. In the long term, when PngCJE is institutionalized by legislation, a permanent structure will be established which will incorporate the establishment of a regional centre for judicial excellence for the Pacific region (‘PicCJE’) in PNG.[/vc_column_text][vc_empty_space height=”1em”][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_custom_heading text=”Board of Directors” font_container=”tag:h3|font_size:36|text_align:center” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][vc_separator color=”custom” border_width=”3″ type=”small” gap=”short” accent_color=”#f27a3e”][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column width=”1/4″][vc_single_image image=”3612″ img_size=”400×500″ alignment=”center”][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1758769442066{margin-top: -20px !important;}”]

title: About the PngCJE
excerpt_text: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_custom_heading text=”About the PngCJE” font_container=”tag:h2|font_size:36|text_align:left” use_theme_fonts=”yes” el_class=”m-b-md”][vc_separator color=”custom” align=”align_left” border_width=”3″ type=”small” accent_color=”#f27a3e”][vc_column_text el_class=”lead m-b-lg”]The Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence (‘PngCJE’) was established in 2010 under a Memorandum of Understanding entered into between the Chief Justice, Chief Magistrate and the Secretary for the Department of Justice and Attorney General.[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/4″][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”3/4″][vc_column_text]The PngCJE is responsible […]
content_html:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_custom_heading text=”About the PngCJE” font_container=”tag:h2|font_size:36|text_align:left” use_theme_fonts=”yes” el_class=”m-b-md”][vc_separator color=”custom” align=”align_left” border_width=”3″ type=”small” accent_color=”#f27a3e”][vc_column_text el_class=”lead m-b-lg”]The Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence (‘PngCJE’) was established in 2010 under a Memorandum of Understanding entered into between the Chief Justice, Chief Magistrate and the Secretary for the Department of Justice and Attorney General.[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/4″]

[/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”3/4″][vc_column_text]The PngCJE is responsible for the delivery of judicial training programs for Judges, Magistrates, Court officers and other officers of the Law and Justice Sector (‘LJS’) agencies that play a part in the Court process. The three key objectives for the establishment of the PngCJE are to:

  1. Promote judicial excellence;
  2. Promote professional development and training; and
  3. Foster an awareness of judicial administration, developments in the law, and social/community issues.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_column_text]The Chief Justice of Papua New Guinea, Sir Gibbs Salika GCL, KBE, CSM, OBE, has been a strong proponent of judicial education locally and regionally and has provided significant resources to develop and grow the PngCJE. The Centre will also be expanding on to conducting and/or coordinating judicial development services for other Pacific island judiciaries should they wish to participate.

The PNG Judiciary through the PngCJE is a major player in the Pacific. It contributes to judicial capacity building in the region through offering judicial education programs to other Pacific jurisdictions.

Apart from being a leader in providing judicial education in the Pacific region, the PngCJE works closely with other judicial training institutions that have similar legal systems or jurisdictions as that of Papua New Guinea. Some of these institutions include the:[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner width=”3/4″][vc_column_text]

  • Commonwealth Judicial Education Institute (CJEI) of Canada;
  • Judicial Commission of New South Wales;
  • Pacific Judicial Strengthening Initiative (PJSI);
  • National Judicial College of Australia (NJCA);
  • Institute of Judicial Studies New Zealand (IJS); and
  • UK Judicial College.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner width=”1/4″]

[/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][/vc_column][/vc_row]

[vc_row wrap_container=”yes” is_section=”yes” remove_margin_top=”yes” remove_border=”yes”][vc_column][vc_custom_heading text=”Welcome to the Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence” use_theme_fonts=”yes” el_class=”m-t-lg m-b-none”][vc_separator color=”custom” align=”align_left” border_width=”3″ type=”small” accent_color=”#f27a3e”][vc_column_text el_class=”m-t-lg”]This is the official website of the Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence (‘PngCJE’). The PngCJE was established in 2010 and coordinates judicial education and training for Papua New Guinea Judiciary, Magisterial Services and the Department of Justice and Attorney General.

The purpose of the PngCJE is to facilitate and coordinate structured professional training to all judicial officers, court officers and officers of the law and justice sector to help improve delivery of judicial services to the people.[/vc_column_text][vc_column_text]Click on the highlighted links to see Video Messages about Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence (PngCJE) from The Chief Justice and Board Chairman of PNGCJE, Sir Gibbs Salika, GCL, KBE, CSM, OBE, and the Judge responsible for PNGCJE, Hon. [vc_row wrap_container=”yes” is_section=”yes” remove_margin_top=”yes” remove_border=”yes”][vc_column][vc_custom_heading text=”Welcome to the Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence” use_theme_fonts=”yes” el_class=”m-t-lg m-b-none”][vc_separator color=”custom” align=”align_left” border_width=”3″ type=”small” accent_color=”#f27a3e”][vc_column_text el_class=”m-t-lg”]This is the official website of the Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence (‘PngCJE’). The PngCJE was established in 2010 and coordinates judicial education and training for Papua New Guinea Judiciary, Magisterial Services and the Department of Justice and Attorney General.

The purpose of the PngCJE is to facilitate and coordinate structured professional training to all judicial officers, court officers and officers of the law and justice sector to help improve delivery of judicial services to the people.[/vc_column_text][vc_column_text]Click on the highlighted links to see Video Messages about Papua New Guinea Centre for Judicial Excellence (PngCJE) from The Chief Justice and Board Chairman of PNGCJE, Sir Gibbs Salika, GCL, KBE, CSM, OBE, and the Judge responsible for PNGCJE, Hon.
